meaning: [史]马其顿王国(古代巴尔干半岛中部一奴隶制国家)
examples: Philip of Macedon wanted to become the master of all Greece.
At least it's a Macedonian name, named after the king of Macedon.
Aristotle was appointed as the head of the Royal Academy of Macedon.
You are also right, I think, to talk of pro- and anti-Alexander (standing for Macedon) factions at Athens.
If we accept Macedon to be the progenitor of his tribe, where is the connection for the rest of the Macedonian tribes?
